To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix and $\lambda$ is a scalar variable.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{pmatrix} $$

To simplify the calculation, let's first find a common denominator for all entries, which is 16. We can rewrite the matrix as:
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix} $$
Let $B = \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix}$. Then $A = \frac{1}{16}B$.
The characteristic polynomial of $A$ is given by $p_A(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$ p_A(\lambda) = \det\left( \frac{1}{16}B - \lambda I \right) = \det\left( \frac{1}{16} (B - 16\lambda I) \right) $$
Since $A$ is a $3 \times 3$ matrix, we can factor out $\frac{1}{16}$ from each of the 3 rows:
$$ p_A(\lambda) = \left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^3 \det(B - 16\lambda I) = \frac{1}{4096} \det(B - \mu I) $$
